properties.name: Ajmer Sharif — Moinuddin Chishti Dargah
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Dargah Committee records

facts: India's most important Sufi shrine — resting place of Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti (1141-1236), founder of the Chishti order in South Asia.
The annual Urs (death anniversary) draws ~500,000 pilgrims across religions — Rajasthan's largest religious congregation after Pushkar.
Every Mughal emperor from Akbar to Aurangzeb paid respects here; Akbar walked barefoot from Agra multiple times.
The dargah's iconic marble dome was built by Humayun; the twin degs (giant cauldrons) cook the tabarruk (blessed food) served to pilgrims.

properties.name: Nagaur — Hameeduddin Chishti Dargah
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Nagaur Dargah records

facts: Dargah of Hameeduddin Chishti (a.k.a. Sultan-ut-Tarkeen) — the first Indian-born khalifa of Moinuddin Chishti, deputed to Nagaur.
One of the earliest Chishti shrines in India (13th c.); the annual Urs is the largest Sufi gathering in the Marwar belt.
Sits below the Ahhichatragarh (Nagaur Fort) — the dargah–fort complex is a UNESCO Asia-Pacific Heritage Award site.

properties.name: Galiakot — Fakhruddin Shaheed Dargah
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + Dawoodi Bohra records

facts: The most sacred pilgrimage site for the Dawoodi Bohra community outside Yemen — resting place of Syedi Fakhruddin Shaheed.
Bohras from across the world visit for the annual Urs; the dargah is maintained by the Dawoodi Bohra da'i-al-mutlaq.
One of only two significant Sufi shrines in the Vagad tribal belt — its patronage brought early Islamic architecture to Dungarpur.

properties.name: Narhar Sharif — Haji Baba Sarkar Dargah

facts: Dargah of Sheikh Hameeduddin Nagori (called 'Haji Baba Sarkar'); a major Qadiri Sufi centre of the Shekhawati belt.
The annual Urs draws Sikh and Hindu pilgrims as well as Muslims — a syncretic 'ganga-jamuni' tradition.
The distinctive white-and-green dargah complex was expanded by the Shekhawati Nawabs in the 18th c.

properties.name: Sarwad Sharif — Hameed Shah Chishti Dargah

facts: Dargah of Hameed Shah Chishti — a khalifa in the Ajmer Sharif lineage; the second-most-visited Chishti shrine in the Ajmer belt.
Located ~80 km southeast of Ajmer; the annual Urs is timed to fall a week after Ajmer Sharif's Urs.
The distinctive octagonal dome is a classical example of 14th c. Rajputana-Islamic fusion architecture.

properties.name: Alwar — Fateh Jung Gumbad + Sufi Complex

facts: Fateh Jung Gumbad — mausoleum of Fateh Jung, a minister in Shah Jahan's court; five-storey octagonal tomb with a Mughal-Rajput fusion.
The adjoining Sufi complex was a major Suhrawardi centre in the Matsya region during the 17-18th c.
Now part of Alwar's protected heritage precinct alongside the Bala Qila and Company Bagh.
